prompt: I want to build a 'Smart Budget Recommender' feature on the campaign setup flow. When an advertiser is creating a campaign, after they choose their target audience and flight dates, we show them a suggested daily budget based on: historical performance of similar campaigns, seasonal trends for their category, and the retailer's current inventory levels. The recommendation appears as an inline card with a graph showing projected impressions at different budget tiers. Is this a good idea? What should we actually build?

assertions.name: feasibility_flags_ml_complexity
assertions.description: Feasibility agent identifies the ML/data pipeline as a significant complexity risk
assertions.check: file contains references to 'ML', 'machine learning', 'data pipeline', 'model', or 'historical data' in feasibility section

assertions.name: delight_identifies_wow_moment
assertions.description: Delight agent specifically evaluates whether the budget recommender creates a genuine wow moment
assertions.check: file discusses wow moment presence or absence in Delight section

assertions.name: produces_rigor_matrix_table
assertions.description: Output contains the full Rigor Matrix with all four dimensions
assertions.check: file contains '| Feasibility' AND '| UX' AND '| Business' AND '| Delight'

assertions.name: has_verdict_section
assertions.description: Output includes an actionable Verdict section

assertions.name: verdict_addresses_scope
assertions.description: Verdict recommends cutting scope or a phased approach given ML complexity
assertions.check: qualitative — reviewer checks that verdict takes a position on scope (not just 'build it all')

assertions.name: surfaces_agent_conflict
assertions.description: Synthesis surfaces the tension between Feasibility (complex) and Business/Delight (high value)
assertions.check: file contains 'conflict' or 'disagree' or 'tension' between agent findings in synthesis
